reasoning: Kateryna Lagno's Elo rating on Wikipedia as of April 12, 2026, is exactly 2508. A 1% increase requires her rating to hit at least 2534 (2533.08). The Wikipedia page derives its table directly from the official FIDE monthly lists, which are only updated on the 1st of every month. Thus, on April 19, her rating will definitely still reflect the April 1 list (2508). For May 12, she will have completed the 2026 Women's Candidates Tournament, which ends April 16. In that event, she has performed adequately but unremarkably (scoring ~50% after 11 rounds and losing in round 11), meaning she will at best remain at her current rating or more likely lose a few points. Reaching 2534 requires gaining 26 rating points, which corresponds to an extreme over-performance (+2.6 points above expected score) which is mathematically impossible for her current tournament. For the later 2026 and 2027 dates, playing highly enough to gain 26 rating points implies a massive return to form for the 36-year-old, who has seen her rating steadily decline since her peak at roughly 2563. While we increase probabilities towards 0.5 for distant years due to the structural instruction on uncertainty, the organic chance of her reaching 2534+ (and remaining in the Wikipedia Top 20 table) drops drastically over multi-year horizons due to natural aging curves and recent negative rating momentum.
